steps.label: Compliance Dashboard Access
steps.description: On this screen, you will log into the system and access the Compliance Dashboard from the main menu. The dashboard provides a centralized location for monitoring compliance metrics across your organization. You will see navigation options and guidance on how to proceed to the dashboard. This is your starting point for compliance oversight activities.
data_validation: User must be authenticated to access the dashboard
User must have Supervisor role to view compliance dashboard

buttons.label: Go to Dashboard Filters
buttons.description: Navigate to the dashboard filter selection screen to begin filtering compliance data.

steps.label: Compliance Dashboard Filter Selection
steps.description: This screen allows you to filter compliance data before viewing the dashboard. Select the Organizational Unit from a dropdown, search for an Individual Worker using autocomplete, specify a Date Range, and choose which Metric Types to display. Use these filters to tailor the dashboard to your needs. Once you have set your filters, click 'Apply Filters' to view the relevant compliance metrics.

fields.label: Metric Type
fields.error_message: Please select at least one metric type.

data_validation: Date Range End must be after Date Range Start
At least one Metric Type must be selected
If Individual Worker is selected, must exist in Workers table

buttons.label: Apply Filters
buttons.description: Apply the selected filters and retrieve compliance metrics for the dashboard.

steps.label: Retrieve Filtered Compliance Metrics
steps.description: Query compliance data sources for the selected organizational unit, worker, date range, and metric types. Aggregate and format the data for dashboard display. Apply access control to ensure the Supervisor only sees authorized data.

rules.label: Filter Data by Selected Criteria
rules.logic: When the Supervisor applies filters, the system queries compliance data sources using the selected values for Organizational Unit, Individual Worker, Date Range, and Metric Type. Only records matching all selected criteria are retrieved.

rules.label: Aggregate and Format Data
rules.logic: The retrieved data is aggregated and formatted for dashboard display. For example, Completed Training is summarized as progress bars, Safety Violations as counts and trends, and Audit Results as pass/fail rates and recent findings.

rules.label: Apply Access Control
rules.logic: Before displaying any data, the system checks the Supervisor's access rights to ensure only authorized data for the selected Organizational Unit and workers is shown. Data outside the Supervisor's scope is excluded from results.

steps.label: Compliance Metrics Dashboard
steps.description: This dashboard displays compliance metrics based on your selected filters. You will see widgets for Completed Training (with progress bars), Safety Violations (counts and trend charts), and Audit Results (pass/fail rates and recent findings). Each widget provides a summary and allows you to drill down for more details. Use the 'View Details' button on any widget to explore specific records and take further action if needed.
data_validation: All displayed data must match the applied filters
Supervisor can only view data for authorized organizational units and workers

buttons.label: View Details
buttons.description: Drill down into the selected metric to view detailed records and take further action.
actions: Navigate to Drill Down to Metric Details

steps.label: Metric Details and Issue Flagging
steps.description: This screen provides a detailed list view for the selected compliance metric. For Completed Training, you will see individual training records per worker. For Safety Violations, incident details are shown. For Audit Results, audit findings are listed. Each record includes a 'Flag Issue' button, allowing you to report compliance concerns directly from the list. Review the details and flag any issues that require follow-up.
data_validation: Each record must be associated with the selected metric type
No duplicate records in the list

buttons.label: Flag Issue
buttons.description: Open the issue flagging form for the selected record.

steps.label: Flag Compliance Issue Form
steps.description: Use this form to flag a compliance issue related to the selected record. The form is pre-filled with context from the record you selected. Choose the Issue Type, provide a detailed Description, and suggest an Action to address the issue. Once completed, submit the form to create a new compliance issue for follow-up by the Compliance Officer.
data_validation: If Issue Type is 'Other', Description must specify the issue

buttons.label: Submit
buttons.description: Submit the compliance issue for processing and assignment.

steps.label: Submit Compliance Issue
steps.description: Validate form input, create a compliance issue record in the database, link it to the relevant worker/metric, and set the status to 'Open'.

rules.label: Validate Form Input
rules.logic: Upon form submission, the system validates all required fields (Issue Type, Description, Suggested Action) are present and correctly formatted. If validation fails, display error messages and prevent submission.

rules.label: Create Compliance Issue Record
rules.logic: If validation passes, the system creates a new compliance issue record in the database, pre-filling context from the selected record (e.g., worker, metric, incident details).

rules.label: Link to Relevant Worker/Metric
rules.logic: The new issue record is linked to the relevant worker and/or compliance metric from which the issue was flagged.

rules.label: Set Status to 'Open'
rules.logic: The status of the new compliance issue record is set to 'Open' upon creation.
